sections.paragraph:
fut. יַעֲמֹד-

(1) to stand. (Arab. عَمَدَ Conj. I. II. IV. transit. to set firmly, to sustain, to prop.) Used of men, Gen 24:30, 31 Gen 24:31 41:17 and of inanimate things, Deu 31:15 Jos 3:16 11:13 . Followed by prepositions-(a) followed by לִפְנֵי to stand before a king, i.e. to serve, to minister to him, Gen 41:46 Deu 1:38 1Ki 1:28 10:8 Dan 1:5 (comp. עָמַד בְּהֵיכַל הַמֶּלֶךְ Dan 1:4); ע׳ לִפְנֵי יְיָ to minister to Jehovah, used of prophets, 1Ki 17:1 18:15 Jer 15:19 priests, Deu 10:8 Jud. 20:28 comp. Psa 134:1. But Lev 18:23, עָמַד לִפְנֵי is used of coition.-(b) followed by עִל-(a) to be set over any one, Num 7:2.-(β) to confide in anything (Syr. ܩܘܶܡ ܠ), Eze 33:26 (γ) to stand by any one, to defend him (comp. עַל No. 2, b), Dan 12:1 Est 8:11 9:16 (comp. קוּם לְ).

(2) to stand, for to stand firm, to remain, to endure (opp. to fall, to perish), ftehen bleiben, beftehn. Psa 33:11, “the decree of Jehovah standeth (for ever).” Psa 102:27, “the heavens shall perish, thou remainest;” Exo 18:23 Amo 2:15 Hos 10:9 Est 3:4. עָמַד בַּמִּלְחָמָה to stand firm in battle; Eze 13:5. Followed by לִפְנֵי to stand firm before any one, to resist him, Psa 76:8 130:3 147:17 Nah 1:6 more rarely followed by בִּפְנֵי Jos 21:44 23:9 נֶנֶד Ecc 4:12 מִן Dan 11:8 simply, Dan 11:15, 25 Dan 11:25followed by בְּ to persist, to persevere, in any thing, Isa 47:12 Ecc 8:3 2Ki 23:3. Once followed by an acc. Eze 17:14, “to keep the covenant (and) to stand to it (לְעָמְדָהּ).” Hence to remain in the same place, Exo 9:28 or state, used both of persons and things, Lev 13:5, 37 Lev 13:37 Jer 32:14 48:11 Dan 10:17 11:6 specially to remain amongst the living, Exo 21:21.

(3) to stand still, to stop, (ftillftehn), as opp. to go on one’s way, to proceed. 1Sa 20:38, “make haste אַל תַּעֲמֹד do not stop.” Used of the sun standing still in his course, Jos 10:13 of the sea becoming tranquil, Jon 1:15 compare 2Ki 4:6. Followed by מִן to desist from any thing, to leave off. Gen 29:35, וַתַּעֲמֹד מִלֶּדֶת “she ceased from bearing children;” 30:9 .

(4) to stand up, arise (aufftehn), i.q. קוּם, but only found in the latter books, Dan 12:1, 13 Dan 12:13often used of a new prince, Dan 8:23 11:2 , Dan 11:3, 20 Dan 11:20 Ecc 4:15 of war springing up, 1Ch 20:4 followed by עַל to rise up against any one, Dan 8:25 11:14 1Ch 21:1 compare Lev 19:16.

(5) pass. to be constituted, set, appointed. Ezr 10:14, יַעַמְדוּ־נָא שָׂרֵינוּ “let our rulers be appointed,” let us appoint our rulers, Dan 11:31.

Hiphil הֶעֶמִיד-

(1) causat. of Kal No. 1, to cause to stand, to set, Psa 31:9 Lev 14:11 used figuratively, to constitute, to decree, 2Ch 30:5 followed by לְ to destine (to promise) to any one, 33:8 followed by עַל to impose (a law) on any one, Neh 10:33 also to constitute, to set in an office or function, 1Ki 12:32 1Ch 15:16.

(2) Causat. of Kal No. 2, to cause to stand firm, or endure, i.e. to establish, to preserve, 1Ki 15:4 2Ch 9:8 Pro 29:4 to confirm, i.q. קִיֵּם 2Ch 35:2 Dan 11:14, “to confirm the vision,” i.e. by the event.

(3) i.q. Kal No. 3, intrans. to stand still, 2Ch 18:34.

(4) to raise, to set up, as statues, 2Ch 33:19 a house, Ezr 2:68 9:9 also, to arouse, to stir up, Neh 6:7 Dan 11:11, 13 Dan 11:13.

Hophal, to be set, placed, Lev 16:10 to remain, 1Ki 22:35.

Derived nouns, עֹמֶד, עֶמְדָה, עַמּוּד, מַעֲמָד, מָעֲמָד.

II.

עמד

ἅπ. λεγόμ. of uncertain authority; Eze 29:7, וְהַעֲמַדְתָּ לָהֶם כָּל־מָתְנַיִם which clearly stands for וְהִמְעַדְתָּ וגו׳ “and thou hast made all their loins to shake;” compare Psa 69:24. But it appears to me uncertain whether the letters are transposed by a certain usage of the language, or whether through some error in this place only. This form however seems to be one of those which are reckoned among the innumerable licenses, or barbarisms [rather peculiarities of dialect] of the prophet Ezekiel.
